column1: # sp0=Cod, sp1=Herring, sp2=Sprat, sp3=Flounder, sp4=Perch, sp5=Pike-perch, sp6=Smelt, sp7=Stickleback
# Growth parameters represent HISTORICAL (pre-2015) Baltic life history.
# Eastern Baltic cod has undergone severe growth impairment since ~2015
# (Svedang et al. 2024, doi:10.1002/ece3.70382): current effective Linf ~60-80 cm,
# current L50 ~20 cm. If modeling post-collapse state, revise cod parameters.
# Herring Linf=27 cm verified against Baltic herring (C. h. membras) literature.
# Sprat Linf=16 cm, K=0.45 consistent with published Baltic sprat data.
# Length-weight allometric coefficients sourced from FishBase (Gadus morhua, Clupea
# harengus, Sprattus sprattus, Platichthys flesus, Perca fluviatilis, Sander
# Length-weight: condition factor (a in W=a*L^b, W in g, L in cm)
# NOTE: Cod L50=38 cm is the HISTORICAL value (stable ~40 cm from 1930s-1990s).
# Current eastern Baltic cod L50 has halved to ~20 cm (Svedang et al. 2024
# DTU data doi:10.11583/DTU:16895254). Revise if modeling post-2015 conditions.
# Relative fecundity (eggs per gram of mature female)
